text.title: Data-Driven Requirements Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real applications of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Requirement evidence cites 'a user complained' instead of an aggregate signal.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats single user feedback as data.
antipattern.fix: Require ≥30 events or ≥2 weeks of signal per requirement; reject single-anecdote evidence.

antipattern.symptom: All requirements ship with confidence=high regardless of sample size.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats confidence as a label, not a calibrated estimate.
antipattern.fix: Tie confidence to sample size buckets (low <100, medium 100-10k, high >10k); auto-downgrade overrides.

antipattern.symptom: Requirement names a target but no baseline metric.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skips the 'where are we now' measurement.
antipattern.fix: Require baseline + target per metric requirement; reject when missing.
